title: I am allowed to do nothing here
subtitle: A present-tense affirmation for giving the body and mind permission to rest without a task.
excerpt: A short affirmation from Day 19 of the SLEEP program. For when the state of doing nothing still feels like a failure rather than a choice.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 19 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025), "Stillness without dread."

content.text: A short affirmation from Day 19 of the SLEEP program. The key word is 'allowed', this is a permission statement before a peace statement.
I am allowed to do nothing here. Stillness is not wasted time. I rest completely and I restore completely.
Say it three times in the evening. On the third pass, sit still for thirty seconds before moving. Let the permission land in the body.
Adapted from Day 19 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
